title: One Card Dungeon - Expansion Preview (and more super small crawls!)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JzsEVr4W5ng

Daniel (@dungeondive) previews the One Card Dungeon expansion (a PDF from the creators, hitting Kickstarter mid-March 2023) and updates his playing-card-sized box of "super small crawls" — which, he's reminded, also holds Mini Rogue, two Advanced Quest modules, and Business Card Dungeon Dive.

The expansion, "The McGuffin Returns," essentially doubles the base game while still fitting the original box. You get a new double-sided dungeon card, four new classes (Necromancer, Cleric, Knight, Rogue — he singles out the Necromancer's spend-life-for-damage and the Cleric's matched-dice luck), a D12 boss die, and a yellow treasure die. The two standout additions: a treasure-chest mechanic (roll a D6, place it opposite the stairs, "fight" it to open — low values are easy but weak loot, high values are tough but powerful, a clever thematic loot system), and bosses every third level (3/6/9/12) fought as extensions of that floor with no healing in between, sharply raising the difficulty.

A neat, compact expansion that keeps the game's tactical-puzzle core intact — and an invitation for viewers to suggest other tiny games that'd fit the box.

----

The treasure-chest "fight it to open it" mechanic is a clever loot solution — what's the best way you've seen a tiny game handle loot?
